# ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0023 - QUIC RFC 9000 Bootstrap Replay ACK Piggyback Architecture

## Purpose

Describe the bounded packet-composition floor that prepends fresh pending Initial ACK frames to ordinary non-Retry bootstrap Initial replay probe packets without changing the retained ClientHello bytes, Initial destination connection ID, empty token, or packet-number continuity.

## Scope

This design covers fresh pending Initial ACK prefixing for ordinary non-Retry bootstrap Initial replay probe packets only. It does not claim Retry-selected replay, candidate-path or multipath scheduling, endpoint interop success, public API behavior, or a general delayed-ACK timer.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0010

## Design Summary

The ordinary client bootstrap probe path continues to replay the retained Initial ClientHello bytes when PTO requires old Initial data and no new client Initial CRYPTO data is buffered. During each replay packet composition, the runtime checks Initial ACK-generation state and formats a fresh ACK frame only when a new ack-eliciting Initial packet is pending acknowledgment. Those transient ACK bytes are passed to the existing Initial packet builder as a prefix before the replayed CRYPTO frame. The protected packet is then tracked under its actual next Initial packet number, and ACK state is marked sent only after tracking succeeds. Later replay fragments skip duplicate ACK prefixes once the pending ACK has been marked sent.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.Protocol.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicHandshakeFlowCoordinator.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P2P1-0010.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/QuicS13AckPiggybackTestSupport.cs
- benchmarks/QuicHandshakePacketProtectionBenchmarks.cs

## Data and State Considerations

ACK prefix bytes are packet-composition data only. They are not stored in the retained ClientHello snapshot, not included in CRYPTO stream replay data, not treated as retransmittable handshake bytes, and not allowed to change the ordinary bootstrap Initial destination connection ID or empty-token header shape.

## Edge Cases and Constraints

- Only ordinary non-Retry client bootstrap Initial probe replay packets emitted from retained bootstrap ClientHello bytes are in scope.
- Retry-selected Initial replay remains owned by ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0022.
- No ACK frame is invented when Initial ACK-generation state has no pending ack-eliciting receipt.
- A sent ACK prefix suppresses duplicate ACK-only or repeated piggyback triggers for the same pending Initial acknowledgment.
- The replayed CRYPTO frame must preserve the retained ClientHello bytes and stream offsets after any ACK prefix.
- The ordinary replay packet must keep the original Initial destination connection ID and an empty token.

## Alternatives Considered

- Treat live Initial CRYPTO ACK piggyback coverage as sufficient; rejected because ordinary bootstrap probe replay uses retained ClientHello bytes after the egress buffer has been drained.
- Reuse the retransmission packet-rebuild path; rejected because ordinary bootstrap replay is driven by the retained bootstrap ClientHello snapshot rather than a sent-packet CRYPTO extraction.
- Widen the slice to delayed-ACK scheduling, path scheduling, or interop success; rejected because those are separate policy and external-runner claims.
- Mark ACK state sent before packet tracking; rejected because suppressed sends must not consume ACK-generation state.

## Risks

- General delayed-ACK timer behavior remains unclaimed.
- Endpoint interop success and public API behavior remain outside this architecture slice.
- Candidate-path and multipath scheduling remain outside this architecture slice.
